title: Family Offices
excerpt:
Family offices are the unsung heroes of early-stage venture capital. Without them, most emerging managers couldn't raise their first fund, and the pre-seed and seed market would be dramatically smaller. But family offices are not VCs—they have different goals, different timelines, and different expectations.

The numbers tell the story. In a typical emerging manager's Fund I or Fund II, family offices provide roughly sixty percent of committed capital. High-net-worth individuals contribute another eighteen percent. Fund of funds add ten percent, institutions contribute eight percent, and other sources make up the balance.

Without family offices, most first-time fund managers simply couldn't exist.
stats: Family offices write checks ranging from $100,000 to $10 million based on relationship and interest; VCs deploy according to defined fund strategy
They invest in a hands-on, angel-like style with typical checks of $250,000 to $2 million
Checks range from $1 million to $10 million if they participate at all
Checks range from $2 million to $20 million
takeaways: GPs should build family office relationships years before you need them and remember that every family office is different—understand their unique situation.
Founders should consider family offices as strategic angels since they often have industry expertise and patient capital.
Everyone should recognize that family offices are the backbone of early-stage venture, even though they rarely get credit for it.
